title: Flagship general agents (Manus, Devin, OpenAI Operator/Agents, Claude Code/computer-use)
focus: What the leading 2026 general-purpose agents actually do well: autonomy, planning, tool use, reliability. What's their secret sauce?

title: Coding / SWE agents (Devin, OpenHands, SWE-agent, Claude Code, Cursor agents)
focus: How top coding agents plan, edit, test, and self-correct across a repo. Patterns Helm's swarm should steal.

title: Multi-agent orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK, Swarm)
focus: Supervisor/worker patterns, handoffs, shared state, parallelism, reflection/verification loops.

title: Agent memory systems (mem0, Letta/MemGPT, Zep, graph+vector memory, context engineering)
focus: How the best agents remember: episodic vs semantic, consolidation, retrieval, forgetting. Upgrade Helm's SQLite memory.

title: Computer-use & browser agents (OpenAI Operator, Claude computer use, Browser Use, Playwright agents)
focus: Reliable GUI/desktop/browser control: grounding, element targeting, verification, recovery. Make Helm's GUI control robust.

title: Planning, reasoning & self-correction (ReAct, Reflexion, Tree/Graph-of-Thought, plan-and-execute, verifier models)
focus: How agents decompose goals, track multi-step plans, check their own work. Upgrade Helm's planning subsystem.

title: Voice & ambient / always-on personal agents (realtime voice, proactive assistants, wearables)
focus: Real-time STT/TTS, proactivity done well, ambient capture. What would make Helm a great always-on personal agent.

title: Tool ecosystems & MCP (Model Context Protocol servers, function calling, integrations)
focus: The best MCP servers / integrations (calendar, email, GitHub, web, data) and tool-use reliability. What Helm should plug in.

title: Agent reliability, evals, cost & safety (guardrails, sandboxing, observability, cost control, agent benchmarks)
focus: How serious agents stay reliable and cheap: error recovery, sandboxes, tracing, budgets, evals. Harden Helm.

title: Frontier agent UX & product patterns (background agents, async tasks, agent inboxes, human-in-the-loop)
focus: How the best agent products feel to use: background/async work, approvals, transparency, handoff. Improve how Helm presents work to the owner.
